pubmed_links: [Nyegaard et al. (2006) Evidence implicating BRD1 with brain development and susceptibility to both schizophrenia and bipolar affective disorder](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16924267/); [Severinsen et al. (2009) Support of association between BRD1 and both schizophrenia and bipolar affective disorder](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19693800/); [Stilling et al. (2016) Identification of the BRD1 interaction network and its impact on mental disorder risk](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27142060/)
summary: BRD1 encodes a bromodomain-containing epigenetic regulator that has been consistently associated with schizophrenia and bipolar affective disorder across multiple independent genetic studies, with mouse models showing cognitive deficits relevant to schizophrenia. (well-established)
